_comment: M2 (code-health review §6): ONE committed ORCID test-vector file, three consumers.
The ISO 7064 MOD 11-2 checksum is reimplemented THREE times (gateway/orcid.py::is_valid_orcid,
portal/add-survey.html::isOrcidChecksum, and the surveys validator's orcid_checksum_ok). This
file is the shared oracle so divergence in any copy goes red. Consumers:
The three impls AGREE on the canonical hyphenated form but DELIBERATELY differ on FORMAT: the
gateway+portal accept a bare 16-char id (no hyphens); the validator additionally accepts a full
https://orcid.org/ URL and rejects the bare form. Each vector therefore carries the set of
consumers whose FORMAT contract covers it in `applies_to`; every listed consumer must return
`valid` for `input`. Checksum-canonical vectors list all three.

vectors.note: canonical valid — the ORCID-documentation example (verified cross-language in C13)
valid with an X check digit (value 10)
same digits, wrong check digit -> invalid (checksum, not format)
wrong check digit where the correct one is X -> invalid
lowercase x rejected (only uppercase X encodes value 10)
X anywhere but the final position -> invalid
empty -> invalid (callers gate emptiness separately; ORCID is optional)
bare 16-char form accepted by gateway+portal; the validator's stricter regex rejects it (format divergence, by design)
